One additional point supporting this decision is that even though other conditions like juvenile idiopathic arthritis may also be associated with vasculitis, Kawasaki disease stands apart due to its unique pattern of arterial involvement specifically targeting smaller and medium-sized arteries during childhood. Additionally, the fact that approximately 25% of untreated cases develop significant cardiovascular sequelae underscores why early recognition and prompt treatment with intravenous immunoglobulin therapy are crucial. These factors make option A not just technically accurate but clinically important.
